task_completion.description: Partition walls are drawn to divide the space into five functional exhibition zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan or 3D view in Sweet Home 3D. Verify that new interior walls have been created to segment the open building shell into at least five distinct areas or rooms.

task_completion.description: Required fixtures and items are placed: at least 8 display fixtures, 6 visitor seating items, 10 lighting fixtures, and 3 decorative/plant items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count the items in the 2D plan or 3D view. Look for at least 8 display items (pedestals, cases, tables), 6 seating items (benches, chairs), 10 lighting items (lamps, track lights), and 3 plants or decorative objects.

task_completion.description: At least 4 text labels are placed to identify the exhibition zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan view for text objects (e.g., 'Main Gallery', 'Gift Shop', 'Sculpture Alcove'). There must be at least 4 distinct text labels present.

task_completion.description: Distinct floor colors or textures are applied to at least 3 rooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Examine the 3D view or the room objects in the 2D plan. Verify that at least 3 distinct rooms have been assigned non-default floor colors or textures.

task_completion.description: The completed project file is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the application title bar for 'gallery_exhibition_starter.sh3d' without an asterisk (which would indicate unsaved changes), or look for a completed 'Save' dialog action in the final screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application window being actively used (menus, catalog, plan view), rather than terminal commands or text editors modifying the .sh3d file directly.

integrity.description: The exhibition layout was built incrementally rather than loaded from a pre-made completed file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence of screenshots should show a gradual addition of walls, furniture, labels, and room colors over time.

integrity.description: The 3D view accurately reflects the items and walls placed in the 2D plan.
integrity.visual_evidence: The bottom 3D pane in Sweet Home 3D should show the rendered walls, furniture, and floor colors corresponding to the 2D layout, proving genuine placement within the software.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses Sweet Home 3D. The starter file is 'gallery_exhibition_starter.sh3d'. The agent must achieve a total of 100 points, with a passing threshold of 70. Required features include wall creation (at least 5 partition walls), furniture placement (8 displays, 10 lamps, 6 seating items, 3 decor/plants), label placement (4 text labels), and room floor color modification (at least 3 rooms with distinct floor styling).
